full_text:
H&E TLS update

26.5.2026

Tested parameters for DBSCAN

Eps_global = 165.2 px = around 40 µm
1 px = 0.2421
min_tls_size = 100

Other tested eps values for 100 samples:
eps_global = 123 (30 um)
eps_global = 143 (35 um)
eps_global = 165.2 (40 um)
eps_global = 175 (42.5 um)
eps_global = 185 (45 um)
eps_global = 196.2 (47.5 um)
eps_global = 205.13 (50 um)

425 tumors 🡪 141 (33%) Agg- // 284 (67%) Agg+

Very poor segmentations 🡪 over 100 TLS

S396_iAdn > vast amount of immune cells

S239_pOme_HE_4 > failed segmentation

Very clear but too loose structures: not detected by DBSCAN (S443_Adn)

Visual vs DBSCAN matching: > 5 groups: 0-2, 3-15, 16-31, 31-60, 61+

Visual vs DBSCAN matching: > 3 groups: 0-2, 3-30, 31+

Visual vs DBSCAN matching: > 3 groups: 0-2, 3-20, 21+

Matching of visual and DBSCAN aggregate counts > tumors divided into two groups based on immune cell proportion (10.39% as a cut-off = median) 🡪 Much better matching in immune-cell low tumors (=tumors with less than 10.39% immune cells from all cells)

Immune cells within Aggs

Each Agg had at least 100 cells
Median: 244 cells

Agg sizes within one patient > samples with at least 2 agg were filtered (242 /284 patients had at least two Agg)

Aggs of each patient randomly splitted into two groups:

Spearman not the best for this 
ICC intraclass correlation coefficient = 0.97: TLS sizes are more similar within the sample than between samples

IC counts and Agg counts grouped

🡪 Higher Agg count correlates with higher EF inflammatory cell count

Agg counts in relation to clinical parameters

🡪 Agg counts higher in omentum

🡪 Not significant

A) CHEMO (chi-square p=0.0591)

B) TISSUE (chi-square p<0.0001)

🡪 Agg counts higher in stage IV tumors

C) STAGE (fischer exact p=0.018)

🡪 Not significant

D) HRD (chi-square p=058)

Agg counts in relation to clinical parameters > histologies

🡪 Not significant differences between different histologies

Agg counts in relation to clinical parameters > BRCA

🡪 Not associated with BRCA status

To be decided

How to group Agg counts for further analyses?
0-2, 3-30, 31+ // 0-2, 3-20, 21+ or something else?

What to do with cases where DBSCAN and visual are unmatching?
Manually checking those falling into different groups/straightly the visual estimate or something else

Do we trust on immune cell phenotyping of segmentation? Can I use it on downstream analyses?
Low/high or low/medium/high grouping 

Downstream analyses with immune cell counts within Aggs? Is it reliable enough?
Which is the best: total count of inflammatory cells or total ef-inflammatory counts?

Next steps

1. Final counts and grouping for Aggs

2. Checking tCycIF and H&E overlapped

3. Final analyses for clinical, molecular and survival associations
PFI and PFS for 3 patients not still updated: S025, S306, S474)

4. Milky spot exploration

t-CycIF analysis direction

B cells are most commonly found in 
GC and Tcell-Bcell areas

Bcell GC/T-Bcell distribution changes across patients

GC

GC + T-Bcell

T-Bcell

Higher infiltrated groups display higher B cell counts

GC RCNs and counts 
do not associate with clinical features

B cells are most commonly found in 
GC and Tcell-Bcell areas

Immune-rich TSI

Fibrotic TSI

Is the TSI composition linked to TLSs in any way?

Sample categorization rationale
TSI % made by Fibrotic and Immune-rich RCNs

SPC

RCN

T-Bcell areas negatively correlate with fibrotic TSI

GC + T-B cells associate with lower fibrotic tsi
